derived.causal_summary: Phase 008 benchmarked the hub's routing (deep-improvement Lane C, router mode) and ran a three-lens family deep-review. The review found zero real router defects: in 9/10 apparent benchmark failures the router selected the correct mode, and the low first-run aggregate (51) was a measurement artifact of scoring a thin hub at the abandoned flat layer plus a scorer that inverted the negative-activation gold. The shared skill-benchmark harness was re-layered (add-only, presence-gated) so a thin hub's resource recall is measured at the surface-router layer, and its negative-scoring was corrected to score suppression scenarios against a real forbidden set while crediting positive-set recall. This took the honest router-mode verdict from a flat-era 44 to 71 (D1-intra 87, D2 79, D3 47, D5 100) with zero net-new test failures versus a pristine-harness baseline. One real P1 merge-blocker was found and fixed: the Iron-Law canary (check-rule-copies.js, CI-wired on PR->main) still pointed at the thin hub SKILL.md and was failing closed; it was repointed to code-verify and its matcher relaxed. Four cheap defects were also fixed. Eight pre-existing flat-layout harness-test failures, the parent-hub-vocab-sync generalization, and the CS-003 matcher are deferred to 009; advisor rebuild + reindex remain deferred to main.
